annotations.label: Zorn's lemma (statement)

anchor.snippet: a partially ordered set containing upper bounds for every chain

annotations.note: `zorn_le` states that if every chain in a preorder is `BddAbove` then a maximal element exists, generalizing the poset statement.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem (application)

anchor.snippet: the Hahn–Banach theorem in functional analysis

annotations.note: `exists_extension_of_le_sublinear` is the Hahn–Banach extension theorem, a key application of Zorn's lemma.

annotations.label: Every field has an algebraic closure

anchor.snippet: every field has an algebraic closure

annotations.note: `AlgebraicClosure k` constructs an algebraic closure of any field `k`, an application of Zorn's lemma.

annotations.label: Well-ordering theorem (equivalent to Zorn)

anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma is equivalent to the well-ordering theorem

annotations.note: The well-ordering theorem (every set admits a well-order) is proved in Mathlib using choice.

annotations.label: Hausdorff maximal principle (earlier formulation)

anchor.snippet: every totally ordered subset of a given partially ordered set is contained in a maximal totally ordered subset

annotations.note: `IsChain.exists_maxChain` says every chain is contained in a maximal chain, exactly the Hausdorff maximal principle.

annotations.label: Partially ordered set

anchor.snippet: is said to be (partially) ordered by

annotations.note: `PartialOrder` is the reflexive/transitive/antisymmetric relation `≤`, the standard poset definition.

annotations.label: Greater than or equal to

anchor.snippet: is said to be greater than or equal to

annotations.note: The relation `a ≥ b ↔ b ≤ a` is core notation `GE.ge` on top of the order `≤` provided by `Preorder`/`PartialOrder`.

annotations.label: Totally ordered set

anchor.snippet: An ordered set in which every pair of elements is comparable is called totally ordered

annotations.note: `LinearOrder` is a partial order in which any two elements are comparable, i.e. a total order.

annotations.label: Inherited order on a subset

anchor.snippet: can itself be seen as partially ordered by restricting the order relation inherited

annotations.note: `Subtype.partialOrder` gives a subset the partial order restricted from the ambient type.

annotations.label: Chain

anchor.snippet: is called a chain

annotations.note: `IsChain r s` is a set pairwise comparable under `r`, exactly the notion of a chain.

annotations.label: Maximal element

anchor.snippet: is maximal (with respect to ≤) if there is no other element of P greater than

annotations.note: `IsMax a` means no element is strictly greater than `a`, the standard definition of a maximal element.

annotations.label: Totally ordered set has at most one maximal element

anchor.snippet: a totally ordered set can have at most one maximal element

annotations.note: No direct lemma found asserting uniqueness of a maximal element in a linear order, though `IsMax`/`IsGreatest` infrastructure exists.

annotations.label: Upper bound

anchor.snippet: an element u of P is an upper bound of S if it is greater than or equal to every element

annotations.note: `upperBounds s` is exactly the set of elements `≥` every member of `s`.

annotations.label: Nonemptiness property is redundant

anchor.snippet: property (1) is redundant, since property (2) says

annotations.note: This is an expository remark about the statement's hypotheses, not a theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inductive set (Bourbaki)

anchor.snippet: a partially ordered set is called inductive if each chain has an upper bound in the set

annotations.note: Mathlib has `ChainCompletePartialOrder` (every nonempty chain has a supremum), related but stronger than Bourbaki's 'each chain has an upper bound'; no named 'inductive set' definition exists.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma fails for a proper class

anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma can fail for a partially ordered class , not a set

annotations.note: This counterexample about proper classes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma (poset form)
annotations.note: `zorn_le` proves existence of a maximal element when every chain is bounded above, stated for preorders (generalizing posets).

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma (inductive set form)
annotations.note: `exists_maximal_of_chains_bounded`, the file's primary form, gives a maximal element whenever every chain has an upper bound.

annotations.label: Corollary (maximal element above a given element)
annotations.note: `zorn_le_nonempty_Ici₀` yields a maximal element `m` with `x ≤ m`, exactly the corollary that a maximal element exists above any given element.

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis

anchor.section: Every vector space has a basis
anchor.snippet: every vector space V has a basis

annotations.note: `Module.Basis.exists_basis` proves every vector space over a division ring has a basis (generalizing fields).

annotations.label: Linearly independent set

anchor.section: Every vector space has a basis
anchor.snippet: all linearly independent subsets of V

annotations.note: `LinearIndependent` is the standard Mathlib predicate for linear independence of an indexed family of vectors.

annotations.label: Nontrivial ring with unity contains a maximal ideal

anchor.section: Every nontrivial ring with unity contains a maximal ideal
anchor.snippet: every nontrivial ring R with unity contains a maximal ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.exists_maximal` shows any nontrivial ring has a maximal ideal (Krull's theorem).

annotations.label: Tychonoff's theorem

anchor.snippet: which in turn implies Tychonoff's theorem

annotations.note: `isCompact_univ_pi` (Tychonoff) proves a product of compact sets is compact.

annotations.label: Finite intersection property

anchor.snippet: with the finite intersection property

annotations.note: Mathlib has no standalone `FiniteIntersectionProperty` definition, but the FIP characterization of compactness is expressed via `CompactSpace.iInter_nonempty`.

annotations.label: Tychonoff's theorem implies the axiom of choice

anchor.snippet: Tychonoff's theorem implies (thus is equivalent to) the axiom of choice

annotations.note: The reverse implication (Tychonoff ⇒ AC) is not formalized; Mathlib assumes choice as an axiom.

annotations.label: Equivalent formulations of Zorn's lemma
annotations.note: The proposition asserting the equivalence of several formulations is not formalized as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inflationary maps have fixed points (equivalent to Zorn)

anchor.snippet: is called an inflationary map

annotations.note: The Bourbaki-Witt theorem `nonempty_fixedPoints_of_inflationary` proves an inflationary map on a chain-complete partial order has a fixed point.

annotations.label: Stronger Zorn's lemma (well-ordered chains)
annotations.note: Mathlib has Zorn requiring all chains bounded; the strengthening that only well-ordered chains need upper bounds is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Hausdorff maximal principle

anchor.section: Proof from the Hausdorff maximal principle
anchor.snippet: is an alternative formulation of Zorn's lemma asserting that

annotations.note: `IsChain.exists_maxChain` is the Hausdorff maximal principle: every chain extends to a maximal chain.

annotations.label: Bourbaki–Witt theorem

anchor.section: Proof from the Hausdorff maximal principle
anchor.snippet: The Bourbaki–Witt theorem can also be used

annotations.note: Bourbaki–Witt: an inflationary map on a chain-complete poset has a fixed point; used here as an alternative route to Zorn's lemma.

annotations.label: Pataraia's theorem

anchor.section: Proof from the Hausdorff maximal principle
anchor.snippet: Pataraia's theorem can be used instead

annotations.note: Pataraia's fixed-point theorem (monotone maps on a dcpo with a least element have a fixed point) does not have a direct Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Lemma 1 (least upper bound form)
annotations.note: This is a proof-internal lemma of a specific Zorn-from-HMP argument, not a standalone Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Lemma 2 (subsets closed under unions)
annotations.note: This is a proof-internal lemma in the article's derivation, not a standalone Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma implies the axiom of choice

anchor.section: Zorn's lemma implies the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma implies the axiom of choice illustrates a typical application

annotations.note: Mathlib takes choice as an axiom and does not formalize the implication Zorn ⇒ AC.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma implies the well-ordering theorem

anchor.section: Zorn's lemma implies the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma implies the well-ordering theorem

annotations.note: The well-ordering theorem `exists_wellFoundedLT` is proved (via cardinals/choice), but the specific implication from Zorn's lemma is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dcpo with least element has a maximal element
annotations.note: Mathlib has general Zorn (`zorn_le`) and chain-complete order infrastructure, but no direct 'dcpo with a least element has a maximal element' lemma.

annotations.label: Equivalence with HMP, axiom of choice, well-ordering

anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma is equivalent (in ZF ) to three main results

annotations.note: The ZF-equivalence of Zorn, HMP, AC and well-ordering is not formalized as an equivalence in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalent to strong completeness theorem

anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma is also equivalent to the strong completeness theorem of first-order logic

annotations.note: The equivalence of Zorn's lemma with the strong completeness theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Banach's extension theorem / Hahn–Banach

anchor.snippet: Banach's extension theorem which is used to prove

annotations.note: `exists_extension_of_le_sublinear` is the Hahn–Banach extension theorem for a functional dominated by a sublinear function.

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis (equivalent)

anchor.snippet: Every vector space has a basis , a result from linear algebra

annotations.note: `Module.Basis.exists_basis` shows every vector space over a division ring has a basis.

annotations.label: Krull's theorem (maximal ideal)

anchor.snippet: Every commutative unital ring has a maximal ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.exists_maximal` (Krull's theorem) gives a maximal ideal in any nontrivial ring.

annotations.label: Tychonoff's theorem (equivalent)

anchor.snippet: Tychonoff's theorem in topology (to which it is also equivalent

annotations.note: `isCompact_univ_pi` proves Tychonoff's theorem; the equivalence with AC itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Every proper filter is contained in an ultrafilter

anchor.snippet: Every proper filter is contained in an ultrafilter

annotations.note: `Ultrafilter.exists_le` shows every proper (`NeBot`) filter is refined by an ultrafilter.

annotations.label: Weak Zorn under ZF+DC (finite chains)

anchor.section: Analogs under weakenings of the axiom of choice
anchor.snippet: any partially ordered set with exclusively finite chains must have a maximal element

annotations.note: Mathlib uses full choice and does not track the ZF+DC weakening for posets with only finite chains.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma for preordered sets

anchor.value: Zorn's lemma — [ 37 ] Let [MATH] be a pr

annotations.note: `zorn_le` is stated under `[Preorder α]`, exactly Zorn's lemma for preordered sets.
